theme: After Hours is a story the office is not fully telling yet -- lock the door, light the candle, let Bloberto speak first
visual_observations: Desktop (1280x800): Vibe is After Hours, Sunday Mar 8, 'Good afternoon, night owl'. Bloberto anchors the center as NIGHT WATCH inside a purple-glow speech bubble. Coffee corner is labeled 'COFFEE (DECAF? JK)' -- the gold standard for floor copy. Five workers at desks (Carlos, Maya, Dave, Sofia, Luna). The office-window at right: 2%, top: 40% renders a prominent ORANGE RECTANGLE -- it is the golden-hour sky gradient (hour >= 17 per WindowElement line 620) with zero label. WindowElement already computes timeLabel ('golden hour') but renders nothing visible. Bottom-center door says '🚪 Entrance' -- entirely ignoring that the vibe is After Hours. The manager desk shows NIGHT WATCH nameplate but has NO desk accessory (on-fire gets ☕, crushing gets 🏆 at Office.jsx lines 1017-1023, after-hours gets nothing). The conference table dark circle sits in the lower-left with one chair rotated 45 degrees -- ambient but unlabeled. The quotes system (quotes.js) has only 4 after-hours lines that loop every 45 seconds -- exhausted in under 3 minutes for a user who keeps the tab open. Bloberto is silent for the first 44 seconds of every page load because the ambient broadcast fires on a 45-second interval. Mobile (375x812): Same state, compressed. Footer tagline 'the servers are watching. go home.' is visible at the bottom.

ideas.title: Window Gets Its Time Caption
ideas.description: WindowElement (Office.jsx lines 602-651) computes `timeLabel` on line 625 -- 'midnight', 'sunrise', 'daylight', 'golden hour', or 'dusk' -- but this string is only used in aria-label on the .office-window div. Nothing visible. In the desktop screenshot the window is an opaque orange rectangle at right: 2%, top: 40% with zero context. Fix: inside WindowElement's return, add a {timeLabel} as a direct child inside .office-window, absolutely positioned. CSS to add at the end of the .office-window block in Office.css: .office-window__time-caption { position: absolute; bottom: 3px; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); font-size: 0.38rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.1em; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.28); white-space: nowrap; pointer-events: none; z-index: 1; }. WHY: The window is the only real-world time artifact on the floor -- WallClock shows vibe-themed hands, but the window literally shows the sky at this hour. Without a label it is decoration. 'GOLDEN HOUR' in tiny ALLCAPS completes the metaphor and earns the element's pixels -- same energy as 'COFFEE (DECAF? JK)' which proves the pattern works.

ideas.title: After-Hours Door Locks
ideas.description: Office.jsx lines 1144-1149: the .office-door div always renders the icon as `doorEvent === 'arrive' ? '🚪✨' : '🚪'` and the label as the static string 'Entrance'. The vibe prop is available in the Office component (line 842). Fix: change the icon expression to `vibe === 'after-hours' ? '🔒' : doorEvent === 'arrive' ? '🚪✨' : '🚪'` and the label string to `{vibe === 'after-hours' ? 'Locked' : 'Entrance'}`. Add one CSS rule in Office.css after .office-door__label (line 382): [data-vibe='after-hours'] .office-door__label { color: rgba(99,102,241,0.45); letter-spacing: 0.14em; }. Also add data-vibe={vibe} to the .office-door div. WHY: The door is the only element on the floor with absolutely zero vibe reactivity. The whiteboard reacts, the coffee reacts, the conference chairs react, Bloberto's nameplate reacts -- but the door always says 'Entrance'. A locked door at midnight makes the After Hours atmosphere narratively complete. This is a 2-line JSX change and 3-line CSS change.

ideas.title: Manager Desk Gets an After-Hours Candle
ideas.description:
Office.jsx lines 1017-1023: the manager desk renders a ☕ coffee accessory for on-fire vibe and a 🏆 trophy for crushing. The pattern is established via .mgr-desk__coffee and .mgr-desk__trophy (Office.css lines 199-218). But after-hours gets nothing -- the desk is empty on its right side while on-fire and crushing both have a bottom-right accent. Fix: add `{vibe === 'after-hours' &&
🕯️
}` to the manager desk JSX block (after line 1023). In Office.css, add the class after .mgr-desk__trophy: .mgr-desk__candle { position: absolute; bottom: 3%; right: 4%; font-size: 0.42rem; opacity: 0.6; animation: candle-flicker 1.8s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes candle-flicker { 0%, 100% { opacity: 0.5; transform: translateY(0); } 45% { opacity: 0.85; } 65% { opacity: 0.4; transform: translateY(-1px); } }. WHY: 'Burning the midnight oil' is already in the vibe-caption copy (line 919). A flickering candle on Bloberto's desk makes that metaphor literal and tangible. 2 JSX lines and 5 CSS lines. After-hours is now the most atmospheric vibe state in the office.

ideas.title: Bloberto Speaks on Mount
ideas.description: In Office.jsx, the Character component's ambient broadcast (line ~432) fires on a 45-second interval starting from mount. This means Bloberto is completely silent for the first 44 seconds of every page load. The speech bubble only appears after nearly a minute. Fix: in the manager variant's ambient broadcast useEffect (starting at Office.jsx line ~420), add a mount-time quote trigger before the setInterval. After prevVibeRef.current = managerVibe (line ~428), add: `const mountQuoteId = setTimeout(() => { if (!isHoveringRef.current) { const qs = VIBE_QUOTES[managerVibe] || VIBE_QUOTES['in-flow']; setBubble({ quote: qs[Math.floor(Math.random() * qs.length)], show: true }); timerRef.current = setTimeout(() => setBubble(b => ({ ...b, show: false })), 4000); } }, 3000); return () => { clearTimeout(mountQuoteId); clearInterval(id); ambientRef.current = null; };`. WHY: First impressions matter. A user who lands on the page during after-hours sees NIGHT WATCH but Bloberto is silent for nearly a minute. A quote appearing 3 seconds after load -- 'Why are you still here? Go home. I mean... I am always here, but you shouldn't be.' -- immediately establishes that the product is alive and opinionated, not just a static dashboard.

ideas.title: Expand the After-Hours Quote Pool
ideas.description: src/utils/quotes.js line ~35: the after-hours array has exactly 4 quotes. The ambient broadcast fires every 45 seconds (Office.jsx line ~432). A user with the tab open for 3 minutes will have heard all 4. The other vibes have 4-5 quotes. Fix: append 4 new entries to the `'after-hours'` array in quotes.js: `'Who approved this deploy at this hour? (It was me. I approved it.)'`, `'Technically tomorrow is a fresh start.'`, `'The on-call rotation shows: everyone. As it should.'`, `'I told the team to go home. I lied to the team.'`. No component changes. WHY: After-hours is the rarest and most emotionally resonant vibe -- somebody is genuinely working late on a Sunday. That deserves the richest quote pool, not the thinnest one. Doubling from 4 to 8 entries keeps the ambient loop feeling fresh through a full work session. This is a pure quotes.js edit with zero risk.

ideas.title: Whiteboard Trails Last Two Vibes as Dots
ideas.description:
The office-whiteboard (Office.jsx lines 988-1011) currently shows only the current vibe text and progress bar. It has no memory. Vibe transitions are visually dramatic (shockwave, caption, nameplate pop) but leave zero trace. Fix: in Office.jsx, add `const vibeHistoryRef = useRef([vibe])` near line 842 (after vibe is computed). Add a useEffect that pushes vibe to vibeHistoryRef when it changes: `useEffect(() => { vibeHistoryRef.current = [...vibeHistoryRef.current.slice(-2), vibe]; }, [vibe])`. Inside the whiteboard JSX (after .wb-progress-label, ~line 1007), add: `
{vibeHistoryRef.current.slice(0, -1).map((v, i) => )}
`. In Office.css: .wb-history-dots { display: flex; gap: 3px; justify-content: center; margin-top: 4px; } .wb-history-dot { width: 4px; height: 4px; border-radius: 50%; opacity: 0.3; } [data-vibe='crushing'].wb-history-dot { background: #a78bfa; } [data-vibe='on-fire'].wb-history-dot { background: #ef4444; } [data-vibe='in-flow'].wb-history-dot { background: #2dd4bf; } [data-vibe='slow-day'].wb-history-dot { background: #9ca3af; } [data-vibe='after-hours'].wb-history-dot { background: #6366f1; }. WHY: The whiteboard is the largest surface area of text on the floor. Right now it shows zero history. Two colored dots below the progress bar tell a story: 'we were in-flow (teal) then on-fire (red), now crushing (purple).' Each dot is 4x4px -- the information density per pixel ratio is exceptional.

ideas.title: Night-Owl Header Greeting Rotates Every Three Minutes
ideas.description: App.jsx line ~480 (the greeting area in the header): The text 'Good afternoon, night owl 🌙' is entirely static for the duration of the session. The 'night owl' label is hardcoded to display when vibe is after-hours or when the user's hour is late. Fix: in App.jsx, add a NIGHT_OWL_LABELS array near the FOOTER_TAGLINES const: `const NIGHT_OWL_LABELS = ['night owl', 'still here?', 'burning the midnight oil', 'technically tomorrow', 'moonlighter', 'the servers see you', 'no shame tho']`. Add `const [owlIdx, setOwlIdx] = useState(0)`. Add a useEffect that only runs when the vibe is after-hours: `useEffect(() => { if (teamVibe.key !== 'after-hours') return; const id = setInterval(() => setOwlIdx(i => i + 1), 180000); return () => clearInterval(id); }, [teamVibe.key])`. Replace the static 'night owl' span with `{NIGHT_OWL_LABELS[owlIdx % NIGHT_OWL_LABELS.length]}` and add a CSS key-reset via `key={owlIdx}` to trigger the existing greeting fade animation. WHY: The header is in frame 100% of the time. A greeting that evolves during an after-hours session -- 'night owl' fading to 'still here?' fading to 'the servers see you' -- plays the user's own marathon back at them. It makes Bloberto feel like a real-time observer, not a dashboard. It fires only during after-hours and only every 3 minutes -- zero noise, maximum delight for the one person who earned it.
